records.section: Section 1 — Your Test Cases and Procedures
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §6 and §11.13, do your test cases and procedures:
records.query: Come from your requirements (requirements-based testing), rather than just from the code?

records.section: Section 1 — Your Test Cases and Procedures
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §6 and §11.13, do your test cases and procedures:
records.query: Include normal-range cases that check the software works as intended?

records.section: Section 1 — Your Test Cases and Procedures
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §6 and §11.13, do your test cases and procedures:
records.query: Include robustness cases that check it behaves safely with invalid, unexpected, or boundary inputs?

records.section: Section 1 — Your Test Cases and Procedures
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §6 and §11.13, do your test cases and procedures:
records.query: Define the test environment, so someone else could reproduce your testing?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.14, do your verification results:
records.query: Show that every planned test, review, and analysis was actually run?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.14, do your verification results:
records.query: Record anything that failed as a problem report, and show it was resolved or dispositioned?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §6.3 and §6.4, have you:
records.query: Reviewed and analyzed the requirements, design, and code (not only tested them) for correctness and consistency?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §6.3 and §6.4, have you:
records.query: Analyzed requirements-based test coverage — that your tests cover every requirement?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §6.3 and §6.4, have you:
records.query: Analyzed structural coverage — that your tests exercised the code to the level your software level demands (statement, decision, or MC/DC)?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §6.3 and §6.4, have you:
records.query: Explained or resolved any code or data your tests did not cover?
Can you trace requirements to test cases to results, so you can show each requirement was verified?
notes: This free checklist is a high-level sanity check — free to use and share. It doesn't include the item-level actions needed to fully close each objective; those live in AeroCert's comprehensive checklist, kept current as the standards change and available from AeroCert. We'd love to hear how it worked for you — tell us at support@aerocert.org.
